skill.name: senior-computer-vision
skill.description: Computer vision engineering guidance for image and video systems, including model training, object detection, inference optimization, dataset pipelines, production deployment, and monitoring practices.
skill.summary: Plan, build, and optimize production computer vision systems.
skill.author: davila7

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found the weak cryptography, SAM database, and network reconnaissance hits are false positives from ordinary computer vision and production wording. The skill contains Python scaffold scripts and documented shell commands, so it carries low operational risk if users run examples without review.

low_findings.title: Static Pattern False Positives in Documentation
low_findings.description: The static weak cryptography, Windows SAM database, and network reconnaissance alerts map to normal documentation terms such as production design, efficient algorithms, resource awareness, and SAM as a computer vision model acronym. No evidence found of cryptographic code, Windows credential access, or network scanning.
low_findings.recommendation: Keep the finding dismissed unless future versions add executable cryptography, credential access, or network probing behavior.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged locations are headings, bullets, or the skill description, not executable code. Nearby context confirms ordinary computer vision and production guidance.

low_findings.title: Manual Shell and Deployment Commands in Documentation
low_findings.description: The skill documents Python, Docker, kubectl, and Helm commands. They are examples in fenced Markdown blocks and are not executed automatically, but users should review targets before running them.
low_findings.recommendation: Publish with a note that command examples are manual and should be adapted to the user environment before execution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The commands are clearly presented as examples in SKILL.md. They can affect local projects or clusters only if a user chooses to run them.

low_findings.title: Placeholder Python Scripts Accept Local Paths
low_findings.description: The Python scripts parse input, output, and config arguments and print JSON status. Review found no subprocess calls, network requests, file deletion, secret access, or automatic execution.
low_findings.recommendation: Treat the scripts as scaffolds and add path validation before expanding them into real file processing tools.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The reviewed script bodies only parse arguments, build an in-memory config, log progress, and print results. No dangerous behavior was present in the flagged sections.

content.value_statement: Computer vision teams need reliable guidance for training, optimization, deployment, and monitoring. This skill provides structured engineering workflows for production image and video AI systems.
actual_capabilities: Guides architecture choices for image processing, object detection, segmentation, and video analysis systems.
Provides reference workflows for dataset preparation, model training, inference optimization, and deployment planning.
Includes scaffold scripts for vision model training, dataset pipeline building, and inference optimization status flows.
Documents production practices for monitoring, reliability, security, scalability, and cost-aware deployment.
Supports discussions about PyTorch, OpenCV, YOLO, SAM, diffusion models, and vision transformers.
limitations: The included scripts are scaffolds and do not implement full training, optimization, or dataset processing logic.
It does not provide pretrained model weights, datasets, benchmark results, or verified production configurations.
Users must adapt shell commands and deployment examples to their own infrastructure.
Security, privacy, and compliance advice remains general and needs project-specific review.

use_cases.title: Design a Vision Model Pipeline
use_cases.description: Plan datasets, training stages, evaluation metrics, and deployment steps for a new visual AI product.

use_cases.title: Optimize Inference Performance
use_cases.description: Review latency, throughput, batching, caching, and model serving choices for a production vision service.

use_cases.title: Standardize Production Practices
use_cases.description: Create team guidance for monitoring, reliability, security, cost control, and operational readiness.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Vision Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me choose a computer vision architecture for [task]. My inputs are [image or video type], constraints are [latency, cost, accuracy], and deployment target is [environment].

prompt_templates.title: Plan Dataset Preparation
prompt_templates.prompt: Design a dataset preparation workflow for [vision task]. Include labeling strategy, data validation, augmentation, train-test splits, and quality checks.

prompt_templates.title: Improve Inference Latency
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this inference pipeline for latency and throughput risks: [pipeline details]. Recommend batching, caching, model format, hardware, and monitoring changes.

prompt_templates.title: Audit Production Readiness
prompt_templates.prompt: Audit this production computer vision system design: [architecture]. Evaluate scalability, observability, failure modes, privacy, security, cost, and rollback strategy.

output_examples.input: Request: Plan an object detection service for warehouse cameras with near real-time alerts.
output: Architecture recommendation with camera ingestion, model serving, event filtering, alert routing, and monitoring stages.
Risk list covering latency spikes, class imbalance, false positives, privacy requirements, and camera outages.
Deployment checklist for validation metrics, canary rollout, drift monitoring, and rollback steps.

output_examples.input: Request: Improve a segmentation model that is accurate but too slow for edge devices.
output: Optimization plan covering model pruning, quantization, input resizing, runtime selection, and hardware profiling.
Measurement plan for comparing accuracy, latency, memory use, device temperature, and failure cases.

output_examples.input: Request: Create a computer vision team checklist before production launch.
output: Checklist covering dataset governance, model validation, observability, incident response, security review, and cost controls.
Ownership matrix for model quality, data pipelines, infrastructure, compliance, and operational support.

best_practices: Start with measurable quality, latency, throughput, privacy, and cost targets before choosing a model architecture.
Validate datasets for coverage, label quality, class balance, and privacy risks before training or evaluation.
Profile the full pipeline in the target environment before committing to an optimization strategy.
anti_patterns: Do not copy deployment commands into production without reviewing namespaces, credentials, and cluster context.
Do not optimize model latency before measuring preprocessing, postprocessing, and I/O bottlenecks.
Do not treat scaffold scripts as complete production tools without adding validation, tests, and error handling.

faq.question: Does this skill train computer vision models directly?
faq.answer: No. It provides guidance and scaffold scripts, but users must implement real training logic and supply data.

faq.question: Which model families does it discuss?
faq.answer: It references PyTorch, OpenCV, YOLO, SAM, diffusion models, vision transformers, and related production patterns.

faq.question: Can it help with real-time inference systems?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes guidance for batching, caching, latency targets, load balancing, monitoring, and deployment tradeoffs.

faq.question: Is it safe to publish in the marketplace?
faq.answer: Yes. Review found low risk because commands are manual examples and scripts do not perform dangerous actions.

faq.question: What should users review before running commands?
faq.answer: Users should verify file paths, deployment context, cluster namespace, credentials, and environment-specific configuration.

faq.question: Who benefits most from this skill?
faq.answer: Machine learning engineers, platform engineers, and technical leads building production image or video AI systems.
